title: Sheeraz vs Eubank Jr: a rising super-middleweight meets a fraying name
summary: Chris Eubank Jr is the bigger name and, at 36, the more vulnerable one. Hamzah Sheeraz is younger, unbeaten and a good deal bigger. This has the shape of a changing of the guard.
body:
Chris Eubank Jr is the bigger name here and, at 36, the more vulnerable one. Hamzah Sheeraz is 27, unbeaten, and a good deal bigger physically. This super-middleweight meeting has the shape of a changing of the guard, provided the younger man is as ready as his record suggests.

Sheeraz: unbeaten, and the longer fighter

Sheeraz arrives 23-0-1 with 19 knockouts, an 83 per cent finishing rate, and a recent five made up almost entirely of stoppages. At 6ft 3in with a 75-inch reach he is the rangier man by a clear margin, the kind of tall, heavy-handed boxer who can fight at distance and still hurt you.

Eubank Jr: experience, and a record that has started to fray

Eubank's case is mileage. His 35-4 record spans 39 professional contests, with 25 knockouts at 71 per cent and 10 decisions, the deep résumé of a fighter who has shared rings with serious operators. His recent five carries both a decision loss and a knockout loss. The experience is real, and so is the wear that has begun to show alongside it.

Size and youth against the name

Sheeraz is four inches taller, three longer in reach, nine years younger and still unbeaten. Eubank brings 39 fights and an awkward durability that has troubled better-credentialled opponents. That experience has to offset a younger, longer, fresher man. On the evidence of his last few outings, the most telling line on the page may be the recent stoppage against Eubank's name.
